2026-03.executiveSummary:
WeTrials is gaining traction on AI capabilities while managing an engagement gap and delivery delays that are being actively addressed.

The platform delivered 3 completed initiatives and 800+ new pediatric cancer studies this month. Monthly Study Finder users grew 34% (179 → 239) and requests up 25% (73 → 91), though engagement depth remains low — pointing to friction in activation and onboarding.

2 of 5 active initiatives are running past target due to new AI workflows and cross-team dependencies; stabilization expected as processes mature. BCC Enrollment Dashboard received positive reception and was presented at their annual meeting. ABTA continues to expand its sponsored studies portfolio and engage new sponsors.

SR&ED FY2025 completed (7 experiments, 43 weeks), freeing capacity for FY2026 development.

insights.title: AI Capabilities Driving Product Positioning
insights.summary: AI Assistant and Medical Analyzer are emerging as core differentiators, shaping both experience and strategic positioning.

insights.title: Engagement Gap Between Acquisition and Usage
insights.summary: User growth is increasing, but low weekly activity indicates friction in converting users into sustained engagement.

insights.title: Delivery Delays Driven by Workflow and Alignment Challenges
insights.summary: New AI-driven workflows and cross-team coordination gaps are impacting delivery speed and execution efficiency.

insights.title: Partner Validation Strong, Execution Dependent
insights.summary: Ongoing collaboration with BCC and ABTA confirms strategic direction, but impact depends on execution readiness and delivery timelines.

insights.title: Content & Data Operations Limiting Scale
insights.summary: Manual validation and content workflows are slowing scalability, highlighting the need for automation and process optimization.

delivery.title: AI Platform Development
delivery.notes:
Beta AI Assistant (Chatbot) – Accessible without registration, guiding users across Study Finder, Learning Center, and Support Programs to improve discovery and initial engagement.

Beta AI Medical Analyzer – Processes and summarizes user medical documents into structured reports. Early engagement is promising, though the email-based flow introduces some drop-off while supporting registration and consent capture.

Overall: Early delivery is underway to validate engagement and guide next-phase improvements, with expected impact on product usage.

delivery.title: Engagement & UX Improvements
delivery.notes:
Video Content Integration to Learning Center 
SEO Enhancements Integration for indexing - Web App 
Platform Enhancements (UX & Stability)

delivery.title: Enrollment Tracking Dashboard
delivery.notes: Study-level tracking implemented; aggregated data layer development in progress.

delivery.title: Sponsored / Featured Study Logic
delivery.notes: Enhancement of sponsored study rotation logic to support visibility and future monetization. Planned Presentation to ABTA April 17, 2026

delivery.title: Data & Content Expansion
delivery.notes: Integrated 800+ studies for pediatric cancer patient resource module

partnerMilestones.milestone: New sponsored studies integration in progress
partnerMilestones.note: New studies added (e.g., Ono Pharmaceutical, Alpheus Medical); ongoing collaboration and content updates.
Enrollment Tracking Dashboard presented at annual meeting; positive feedback received.
Go-live plan communicated; timeline dependent on video integration and final validation.

comingMonthItems.text: Enrollment Dashboard (BCC) - Review and Aggregated data layer live; BCC sign-off received
Media and video integration into study details

risks.mitigation: Onboarding flow redesign scoped for May; AI Assistant tooltip prompts shipping week of Apr 20; target 30% activation rate by end of Q2
Daily team sync reinstated; scope frozen; backlog items deprioritized to protect core delivery.

items.text: Preparation for the SR&ED FY2026 submission is in progress, including consolidation of technical documentation, experimental narratives, and alignment with reporting requirements.
Preparation for the AI Caregiver Challenge is underway, focusing on defining solution scope, aligning with program requirements, and positioning WeTrials’ capabilities for submission.
